scores.correctness_notes: Response covers the ops/Python client perspective well but misses key gold standard elements: CLI commands (pebble changes, pebble tasks), REST API endpoints (GET /v1/changes/{id}, GET /v1/changes/{id}/wait, GET /v1/changes?select=in-progress), and the notices/change-update mechanism. The state persistence to $PEBBLE/.pebble.state is also absent.
scores.hallucination_notes: The claim that Container methods like replan(), start(), stop(), restart() return a ChangeID is inaccurate — in the ops library these methods block and return None by default, not a ChangeID. The lower-level pebble Client methods return ChangeIDs but the Container convenience methods do not. Also container.pebble.get_change() and container.pebble.wait_change() — the attribute is container.get_plan() etc; the pebble client is accessed differently. Minor inaccuracies rather than fully fabricated APIs.
